name: Glen's "Green Floater" Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds lean steak, cut into 1/4-inch cubes
9 cans (15-oz) dark red kidney beans, drained
9 cans (15-oz) light red kidney beans, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the meat in a LARGE pot.
2. Add garlic and start chopping vegetables in the order listed below, adding each to the pot as you chop.
- Bell peppers (whole)
- Onions
- Tomatoes
- Mushrooms
- Serrano chiles (whole, stemmed)
- Jalapeno chiles (whole, stemmed)
3. Stir after each addition.
4. Add the juice from the canned tomatoes and the chopped canned tomatoes.
5. Simmer all ingredients for 2-3 hours.
6. Add the beans and simmer an additional 30 minutes to heat the beans.
7. Serve hot, and add a "floater" or two for those who like their food extra spicy.
8. If at any point the mixture starts to overflow, divide it between two pots.
9. Adjust seasonings to your taste; adding more ground red pepper will increase spiciness.
Note: Be cautious with chiles that have thin skins, as they may break apart during cooking and make the chili excessively hot. The large green chiles are not ideal for this dish due to their tough skins and mild flavor.
